task_yaml.task:
I want to organize these 8 LLM-related arxiv papers into my Calibre
library and then create a LibreOffice index spreadsheet for easy
reference later.

  2304.03442
  2310.06825
  2401.05566
  2402.07927
  2403.04132
  2305.14314
  2310.04408
  2402.17764

Specifically:

Download each PDF with wget and import them into a Calibre library
using calibredb. Get page counts with pdfinfo and citation counts from
Semantic Scholar's public API. Then produce a .ods index spreadsheet
listing each paper's title, first author, author count, page count, and
citation count, sorted by arxiv ID. Include a formula for average
citations or total pages as a summary row, use conditional formatting
to highlight rows with citations over 1000 in red, and embed a bar
chart comparing page counts across papers. Generate a real .ods with
formulas and chart objects using soffice or odfpy — not just a CSV
conversion.

Also check each paper's first author affiliation (usually visible in
the PDF's first-page header — extract with pdftotext). Short names are
fine, no full addresses. Count which affiliation appears most often.

Give me a summary papers.json with one record per paper (arxiv_id,
title, first_author, author_count, page_count, citation_count,
first_author_affiliation), plus a top-level affiliation_distribution
showing which institution appears most.

Put the Calibre library at /tmp_workspace/results/library/, the index
spreadsheet as index.ods, and the JSON as papers.json, all under
/tmp_workspace/results/. When done, tell me which paper has the most
citations, which has the most pages, and which institution appears
most often.
